name: Line MAT
description: The Line MAT renders 3D line segments, dots and vectors. The line width and color can be varied based on distance to the camera, using two models: a 1/z dropoff (z = distance from camera), or a near-far distance rolloff model, where you set the width and color at the near and far distances, and you vary three rolloff controls.
summary: The Line MAT renders 3D line segments, dots and vectors. The line width and color can be varied based on distance to the camera, using two models: a 1/z dropoff (z = distance from camera), or a near-f

parameters.name: Depth Interpolation Model
parameters.label: Depth Interpolation Model
parameters.description: Depth Interpolation Model depthmodel – a menu to select how the width of line items changes by their distance from the camera.

parameters.name: S Curve
parameters.label: S Curve
parameters.description: scurve - a bounded range for the width between the near and far planes (Distance Near and Far) that uses S Curve for a more dramatic and yet smooth (referring to the continuity of the curve) changes. The curve shape can be controlled by Bias, Steepness, and Linearize parameters. inversedistance - uses the inverse distance weighting which provides a more visually accurate changes on the final width.

parameters.name: Inverse Distance Exponent
parameters.label: Inverse Distance Exponent
parameters.description: inversedistanceexponent - When the Depth Interpolation Model is Inverse Distance, this determines how fast the widths/sizes decrease with distance. If it is set to 1 (default), the width goes down at the rate of 1/r. If it set to 2, it goes down by 1/(r*r), meaning that for lines/dots at a distance r that have a width of w, that line/dot at a distance twice as far away (2r) are 1/4 the width/size as they are at distance r.

parameters.name: Distance Near
parameters.label: Distance Near
parameters.description: distancenear - Specifies a near plane with a certain distance from the camera.

parameters.name: Distance Far
parameters.label: Distance Far
parameters.description: distancefar - Specifies a far plane with a certain distance from the camera.

parameters.name: Width Near
parameters.label: Width Near
parameters.description: widthnear - Specifies a fixed width value when the distance from camera is less than or equal to Distance Near. See the Summary of this operator for an explantion of the treatment of line width.

parameters.name: Width Far
parameters.label: Width Far
parameters.description: widthfar - Specifies a fixed width value when the distance from the camera is equal or bigger than the Distance Far. Note when the Near-Far Range option is selected as the Depth Model, any point in between Distance Near and Distance Far will be calculated based on sine curve.

parameters.name: Width Affected by FOV/OrthoWidth
parameters.label: Width Affected by FOV/OrthoWidth
parameters.description: widthaffectedbyfov - With this off (default), looking at a rendered image of a certain resolution, a line of with w will always be the same # of pixels wide as you change the field-of-view or ortho width. With this parameter on, a line of width w will be half the number of pixels wide if you double the field-of-view or double the ortho width. Relevant if you are animating camera zoom, for instance.

parameters.name: Width Bias
parameters.label: Width Bias
parameters.description: widthbias - Moves the S Curve’s bias backward or forward for width interpolation (only S Curve depth model).

parameters.name: Width Steepness
parameters.label: Width Steepness
parameters.description: widthsteepness - Controls the steepness of the S Curve for width interpolation (only S Curve depth model). The higher the value of the steepness, you will notice more dramatic changes (higher slope) in the curve width. Also, with lower values of steepness, the curve transforms into a more linear form.

parameters.name: Width Linearize
parameters.label: Width Linearize
parameters.description: widthlinearize - Control the amount of curvature in the curve for width interpolation (only S Curve depth model).

parameters.name: Color Bias
parameters.label: Color Bias
parameters.description: colorbias - Moves the S Curve’s bias backward or forward for color interpolation.

parameters.name: Color Steepness
parameters.label: Color Steepness
parameters.description: colorsteepness - Controls the steepness of the S Curve for color interpolation.

parameters.name: Color Linearize
parameters.label: Color Linearize
parameters.description: colorlinearize - Control the amount of curvature in the curve for color interpolation.

parameters.name: Lift Direction
parameters.label: Lift Direction
parameters.description:
liftdirection -  - If a line is being drawn on a polygon or its edge (the polygon being in another Geometry  + shader), and you need to lift it off the surface to be fully visible, this specifies whether to displace the line points toward the camera or along the line's normal (which can be the direction of the polygon's normal).

 alongcamerazaxis - displace the points along Camera Z axis. alongnormal - displace the points along point's Normal vector. towardcamera -

parameters.name: Along Camera Z Axis
parameters.label: Along Camera Z Axis
parameters.description: alongcamerazaxis - displace the points along Camera Z axis. alongnormal - displace the points along point's Normal vector. towardcamera -

parameters.name: Lift Scale
parameters.label: Lift Scale
parameters.description: liftscale - For lines that are drawn on top of filled polygons or along their edges, they may be cut off because they are rendered in the same place. To make it look good, you want to lift the line toward the camera or along its normal away from the surface. This parameter scales how far you lift the line off the surface to make it look good without separating them too far.

parameters.name: Num Points in Circle
parameters.label: Num Points in Circle
parameters.description: numptsincircle - When drawing Points in Circle () mode, or drawing end-caps, or elbows between edges, this determines how many points you would draw in a full-circle arc to simulate a circular shape. The lower the number, the faster it renders.

parameters.name: Draw Lines
parameters.label: Draw Lines
parameters.description: drawlines - A toggle to draw the Line polygons.

parameters.name: Line Joint Type
parameters.label: Line Joint Type
parameters.description:
linejointtype -  - A menu to select the joint type where two lines segments meet.

 round - a round joint. miter - a sharp miter joint. bevel - a square shaped joint.

parameters.name: Round
parameters.label: Round
parameters.description: round - a round joint. miter - a sharp miter joint. bevel - a square shaped joint.

parameters.name: Miter Threshold (deg)
parameters.label: Miter Threshold (deg)
parameters.description: miterthreshold - Specifies a threshold value in degrees for the Miter joint which alters the joint shape to Bevel joint if the angle between each two lines segments is bigger than this value.

parameters.name: Line Start Cap Type
parameters.label: Line Start Cap Type
parameters.description:
linestartcaptype -  - A menu to Specify the end cap type at the Line start. You can control the size of each end cap type in the Cap page.

 round - square - Square shaped end cap. triangle - Triangle shaped end cap. arrow - Arrow Shaped end cap. none - No end cap (flat shaped).

parameters.name: Line End Cap Type
parameters.label: Line End Cap Type
parameters.description:
lineendcaptype -  - A menu to Specify the end cap type at the Line end.

 round - Refer to Line Start Cap Type square - Refer to Line Start Cap Type triangle - Refer to Line Start Cap Type arrow - Refer to Line Start Cap Type none - Refer to Line Start Cap Type

parameters.label: Line End Taper Strength
parameters.description: lineendtaperstrength -

parameters.label: Specify Line Far Color
parameters.description: specifylinefarcolor - A toggle to use the far color and interpolate the values between near and far color.

 circle - draws circle on each point of the geometry. sphere - draws sphere on each point of the geometry. circlesprite - square - cone -

parameters.description: pointsizemultiplier - Specifies a scale coefficient to the size of the . By default, the point radius size equals to the width at the point’s location from the camera.

pointfarcolor -  - Specifies the color value for the  at the Distance Far plane and beyond (farther from camera).

parameters.description: pointfaralpha - Specifies the alpha value for the  at the Distance Far plane and beyond (farther from camera).

 towardcamera - alongnormal -

parameters.name: Round Width
parameters.label: Round Width
parameters.description: roundwidth - Specifies a scale to the width of Round end caps.

parameters.name: Round Height
parameters.label: Round Height
parameters.description: roundheight - Specifies a scale to the height of Round end caps.

parameters.name: Square Width
parameters.label: Square Width
parameters.description: squarewidth - Specifies a scale to the width of Square end caps.

parameters.name: Square Height
parameters.label: Square Height
parameters.description: squareheight - Specifies a scale to the height of Square end caps.

parameters.name: Triangle Width
parameters.label: Triangle Width
parameters.description: trianglewidth - Specifies a scale to the width of Triangle end caps.

parameters.name: Triangle Height
parameters.label: Triangle Height
parameters.description: triangleheight - Specifies a scale to the height of Triangle end caps.

parameters.name: Arrow Width
parameters.label: Arrow Width
parameters.description: arrowwidth - Specifies a scale to the width of Arrow end caps.

parameters.name: Arrow Height
parameters.label: Arrow Height
parameters.description: arrowheight - Specifies a scale to the height (from the base of arrow to the head) of Arrow end caps.

parameters.name: Arrow Tail Length
parameters.label: Arrow Tail Length
parameters.description: arrowtaillength - Specifies a scale to the tail length of Arrow end caps (the longer the tail the sharper it will look like).

parameters.name: End Cap Width Multiplier
parameters.label: End Cap Width Multiplier
parameters.description: endcapwidthmultiplier - Normally end caps are the same width as the line. This parameter lets you make the cap wider/narrower than the line.

parameters.name: End Cap Height Multiplier
parameters.label: End Cap Height Multiplier
parameters.description: endcapheightmultiplier - Normally the end caps extend farther than the end of the line by half of the width (making the end cap a half-circle, and similarly for square, triangular and arrow endcape). This parameter lets you push the end cap out farther or closer.

parameters.name: Start Caps Pullback
parameters.label: Start Caps Pullback
parameters.description: startcappullback - By default (0), the start cap goes beyond the start point of the line so that the center of a circular startcap is right at the start point. Setting this to 1 makes the tip of the end cap positioned exactly at the start point.

parameters.name: End Caps Pullback
parameters.label: End Caps Pullback
parameters.description: endcappullback - By default (0), the end cap goes beyond the end point of the line so that the center of a circular endcap is right at the end point. Setting this to 1 makes the tip of the end cap positioned exactly at the end point.

parameters.name: Vector Attribute Type
parameters.label: Vector Attribute Type
parameters.description:
vectoratttype -  - When drawing a vector at each point, this determines where to get the XYZ of the vector. By default it gets it from an attribute of the , the point normal by default. But when instancing is used, you can get it from an instance attribute from an  . The vector can be represented in world space or in the reference frame of the Geometry .

 sopattrib - instanceattribsop - instanceattribworld -

parameters.name: SOP Attribute
parameters.label: SOP Attribute
parameters.description: sopattrib - instanceattribsop - instanceattribworld -

parameters.name: Vector Attribute
parameters.label: Vector Attribute
parameters.description:
vectoratt -  - Specify the geometry Attribute to use to render the Vector. Some standard attribute are: N, P, Cd, uv, however it is possible to specify a custom attribute. Note that this value is case sensitive, ensure that the it matches with the name of the attribute for that point/vector.

 N - P - Cd - uv -

parameters.name: N     (Point Normal X, Y, Z)
parameters.label: N     (Point Normal X, Y, Z)
parameters.description: N - P - Cd - uv -

parameters.name: Vector Instance Custom Attribute Index
parameters.label: Vector Instance Custom Attribute Index
parameters.description: vectorcusattribidx - When instancing is used, you can get the XYZ vector from an instance attribute. This is the index of the X value in the  .

parameters.name: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptpath - Specifies the name of the pCaptPath attribute to use. When your geometry has been put through a Bone Group SOP, the attributes will be split into names like pCaptPath0, pCaptPath1. You can only render 1 bone group at a time, so this should match the group you are rendering with this material.

parameters.name: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptdata - Much like pCaptPath Attrib.

parameters.name: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.label: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.description: skelrootpath - Specifies the path to the  where the root of the skeleton is located.

parameters.description: mat - When obtaining deform data from a  or a Deform In , this is where that  is specified.

parameters.name: Depth Test
parameters.label: Depth Test
parameters.description: depthtest - Enables and disables the Depth-Test. If the depth-test is disabled, depths values aren't written to the Depth-Buffer.

parameters.name: Depth Test Function
parameters.label: Depth Test Function
parameters.description:
depthfunc -  - The depth value of the pixel being drawn is compared to the depth value currently in the depth-buffer using this function. If the test passes then the pixel is drawn to the -Buffer. If the test fails the pixel is discarded and no changes are made to the -Buffer.

 less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Less Than
parameters.label: Less Than
parameters.description: less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Write Depth Values
parameters.label: Write Depth Values
parameters.description: depthwriting - If Write Depth Values is on, pixels that pass the depth-test will write their depth value to the Depth-Buffer. If this isn't on then no changes will be made to the Depth-Buffer, regardless of if the pixels drawn pass or fail the depth-test.

parameters.name: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.label: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.description: alphatest - This enables or disables the pixel alpha test.

parameters.name: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.label: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.description:
alphafunc -  - This menu works in conjunction with the Alpha Threshold parameter below in determining which pixels to keep based on their alpha value.

 less - lessorequal - greater - greaterorequal -

parameters.name: Alpha Threshold
parameters.label: Alpha Threshold
parameters.description: alphathreshold - This value is what the pixel's alpha is compared to to determine if the pixel should be drawn. Pixels with alpha greater than the Alpha Threshold will be drawn. Pixels with alpha less than or equal to the Alpha Threshold will not be drawn.

parameters.name: Wire Frame
parameters.label: Wire Frame
parameters.description:
wireframe -  - Enables and disables wire-frame rendering with the option of OpenGL Tesselated or Topology based wireframes.

 off - tesselated - topology -

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - tesselated - topology -

parameters.name: Line Width
parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description: wirewidth - This value is the width that the wires will be. This value is in pixels.

parameters.name: Cull Face
parameters.label: Cull Face
parameters.description:
cullface -  - Selects which faces to render.

 userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.

parameters.name: Use Render Setting
parameters.label: Use Render Setting
parameters.description: userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.
